original:
‡ pallāṇḍu pallāṇḍu pallāyirattāṇḍu ⋆
pala kōḍi nūṟāyiram ⋆
mallāṇḍa tiṇdōḻ maṇivaṇṇā ! ⋆
un śēvaḍi śevvi tirukkāppu
explanation: Blessing of long life (Pallandu) for thousands of years, and millions of years, to the Lord with strong shoulders who wrestled and won, whose complexion is like a dark gem. May your red lotus feet be protected.

original:
‡ aḍiyōmōḍum ninnōḍum ⋆
pirivinṟi āyiram pallāṇḍu ⋆
vaḍivāy nin vala mārbinil ⋆
vāzginṟa maṅgaiyum pallāṇḍu ⋆
vaḍivār śōdi valattuṟaiyum ⋆
śuḍarāziyum pallāṇḍu ⋆
paḍai pōr pukku muzaṅgum ⋆
appāñcaśanniyamum pallāṇḍē
explanation: May we, your devotees, remain united with you without separation for thousands of years. May the Goddess Lakshmi, who resides on the right side of your chest, live for many years. May your radiant Sudarshana discus reside on your right hand for many years. May your Panchajanya conch, which sounds in battle, live for many years.

original:
vāzāṭ paṭṭu ninṟīr uḻḻīrēl ⋆
vandu maṇṇum maṇamum koṇmin ⋆
kūzāṭ paṭṭu ninṟīrgaḻai ⋆
eṅgaḻ kuzuvinil pugudaloṭṭōm ⋆
ēzāṭ kālum pazippilōm nāṅgaḻ ⋆
irākkadar vāz ⋆
ilaṅgai pāzāḻāga ppaḍai porudānukku ⋆
pallāṇḍu kūṟudumē
explanation: If you wish to serve the Lord forever, come and join us. We will not allow those who seek only material wealth to enter our group. We, free of blame for seven generations, sing Pallandu to the Lord who destroyed Lanka, where the demons lived.

original:
periyāzvār tirumozi 1.1 – tiruppallāṇḍu
ēḍu nilattil iḍuvadan munnam vandu ⋆
eṅgaḻ kuzām pugundu ⋆
kūḍu manam uḍaiyīrgaḻ varambozi ⋆
vandollai kkūḍuminō ⋆
nāḍu nagaramum nangaṟiya ⋆
namō nārāyaṇāyavenṟu ⋆
pāḍu manam uḍai ppattar uḻḻīr ! ⋆
vandu pallāṇḍu kūṟuminē
explanation: Before your bodies are buried or cremated, join our assembly if you have a willing heart. Leave your limitations behind. Devotees who sing 'Namo Narayanaya' so that the whole country and cities know, come and sing Pallandu!

original:
aṇḍakkulattukkadibadi āgi ⋆
aśurar irākkadarai ⋆
iṇḍai kkulattai eḍuttu kkaḻainda ⋆
iruḍīgēśan tanakku ⋆
toṇḍa kkulattil uḻḻīr ! vandaḍi tozudu ⋆
āyira nāmam śolli ⋆
paṇḍai kkulattai ttavirndu ⋆
pallāṇḍu pallāyirattāṇḍenminē
explanation: To Hrishikesha, the Lord of the universe, who completely uprooted the hosts of asuras and rakshasas, all of you in the lineage of devotees, come and bow at His feet, chant His thousand names, discard your old associations, and sing Pallandu!

original:
endai tandai tandai tandai tam mūttappan ⋆
ēz paḍi kāl toḍaṅgi ⋆
vandu vazivazi āṭceyginṟōm ⋆
tiruvōṇa ttiruvizavil andiyam pōdil ari uruvāgi ⋆
ariyai azittavanai ⋆
pandanai tīra ppallāṇḍu ⋆
pallāyirattāṇḍenṟu pāḍudumē
split_lines: endai | tandai | tandai | tandai | tam | mūttappan
tiruvōṇa | ttiruvizavil | andiyam | pōdil | ari | uruvāgi
explanation: For seven generations, my father, his father, and ancestors have come to serve the Lord. To Him who appeared as Narasimha at dusk during the Sravanam festival and destroyed Hiranyakashipu, we sing Pallandu to destroy all bondages.

original:
tīyiṟ poliginṟa śeñjuḍar āzi ⋆
tigaz tiruccakkarattin ⋆
periyāzvār tirumozi 1.1 – tiruppallāṇḍu
kōyiṟpoṟiyālē oṭruṇḍu ninṟu ⋆
kuḍikuḍi āṭceyginṟōm ⋆
māya pporupaḍai vāṇanai ⋆
āyiran tōḻum pozi kurudi pāya ⋆
śuzaṭriya āzi vallānukku ⋆
pallāṇḍu kūṟudumē
explanation: Branded by the divine mark of the glowing Sudarshana discus, which shines brighter than fire, generation after generation we perform service. To the Lord who chopped off the thousand arms of Banasura, we sing Pallandu.

original:
neyyiḍai nalladōr śōṟum ⋆
niyatamum attāṇi ccēvakamum ⋆
kaiyaḍai kkāyum kazuttukku ppūṇoḍu ⋆
kādukku kkuṇḍalamum ⋆
meyyiḍa nalladōr śāndamum tandu ⋆
ennai veḻḻuyir ākka valla ⋆
paiyuḍai nāga ppagaikkoḍiyānukku ⋆
pallāṇḍu kūṟuvanē
explanation: The Lord whose flag bears Garuda grants me good rice mixed with ghee, daily service, betel leaves, neck ornaments, earrings, and sandalwood paste to purify my soul. To Him, I sing Pallandu.

original:
uḍuttu kkaḻainda nin pīdagavāḍai uḍuttu ⋆
kalattaduṇḍu ⋆
toḍutta tuzāymalar śūḍi kkaḻaindana ⋆
śūḍumittoṇḍargaḻōm ⋆
viḍutta tiśai kkarumam tirutti ⋆
tiruvōṇa ttiruvizavil ⋆
paḍutta painnāgaṇai ppaḻḻikoṇḍānukku ⋆
pallāṇḍu kūṟudumē
explanation: The Lord gives us yellow silk robes to wear, His prasadam to eat, betel leaves, and flower garlands for our shoulders, removing our sorrows. To the mysterious Lord wearing honey-dripping Tulasi garlands, who is sweet to my heart, we sing Pallandu.

original:
ennāḻ emberumān ⋆
undanakkaḍiyōm enṟezuttuppaṭṭa annāḻē ⋆
aḍiyōṅgaḻ aḍikkuḍil ⋆
periyāzvār tirumozi 1.1 – tiruppallāṇḍu
vīḍu peṭruyndadu kāṇ ⋆
śennāḻ tōṭri ⋆
tirumaduraiyuḻ śilaikunittu ⋆
aindalaiya painnāga ttalai pāyndavanē ! ⋆
unnai ppallāṇḍu kūṟudumē
explanation: According to the palm-leaf record that we are your eternal servants, and the deed stating we perform service to your devotees, the Lord has freed us from worldly desires. To Madhava, my eternal Master, I sing Pallandu.

original:
‡ alvazakkonṟum illā ⋆
aṇi kōṭṭiyar kōn ⋆
abimāna tuṅgan śelvanaippōla ⋆
tirumālē ! nānum unakku ppazavaḍiyēn ⋆
nalvagaiyāl namō nārāyaṇāvenṟu ⋆
nāmam pala paravi ⋆
palvagaiyālum pavittiranē ! ⋆
unnai ppallāṇḍu kūṟuvanē
explanation: Those who sing Pallandu to the faultless Lord Vishnu will be praised by all. They will have no obstacles or questions in their path, and they will directly enter the eternal kingdom of Sri Vaikunta to serve the wealthy Lord.

original:
‡ pallāṇḍenṟu pavittiranai ⋆
paramēṭṭiyai ⋆
śārṅgam ennum villāṇḍān tannai ⋆
villiputtūr viṭṭuśittan virumbiya śol ⋆
nallāṇḍenṟu navinṟuraippār ⋆
namō nārāyaṇāyavenṟu ⋆
pallāṇḍum paramātmanai ⋆
śūzndirundēttuvar pallāṇḍē
explanation: These auspicious words were composed by Vishnuchitta (Periyalvar) of Srivilliputhur in praise of the pure Lord who wields the Sarnga bow. Those who recite these verses, chanting 'Namo Narayanaya', will surround the supreme Lord and sing Pallandu for eternity.
